turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Gorsuch has the opinion of the Court this morning in case 22-200 Slack Technologies v. Pirani.

turns.name: JUSTICE GORSUCH
turns.text: This case concerns one very narrow provision of the Federal Securities Laws. Section 11 of the Securities Act of 1933 imposes strict liability on companies that issue shares pursuant to materially false or misleading registration statements. For years, lower courts have understood this provision to apply only to shares issued pursuant to a registration statement. At the same time, lower courts have recognized that other provisions of the Securities Laws, most notably Section 10(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 allow investors to sue for fraud and the sale of unregistered shares on proof of scienter. Recently, the Ninth Circuit parted ways with other lower courts and held that Section 11 of the 33 Act applies even to unregistered shares. We agreed to review that decision to ensure uniform application of federal law. For reasons we detail in our opinion today, we reject the Ninth Circuit's reading and align ourselves with all the other Courts of Appeals to have addressed Section 11 scope. The decision of the court is unanimous.
